content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Wet Shark and Blocks". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/621/problem/E
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of blocks. Then, n lines follow, each containing an integer ai, which is the height of the ith block.
content.outputFormat: The output should be the minimum number of moves required to make all blocks have the same height.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the array of block heights and then find the median. The minimum number of moves is the sum of the absolute differences between each height and the median.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = [int(input()) for _ in range(n)]
arr.sort()
median = arr[n // 2]
moves = sum(abs(x - median) for x in arr)
print(moves)
